# Solar Workspace

## Deployment steps
### SQL Server 
1. Enable Cloud SQL Admin API in the Google Cloud Console.
2. To the service account `848723212231-compute@developer.gserviceaccount.com`, assign the role `Cloud SQL Client`.
3. Added secrets to the github actions:
    * `SQL_CONNECTION_NAME_DEV`: found in the instance overview page ('shidav-solar-dev:me-west1:shidav-solar-dev' for dev)
    * `SQL_USER_DEV`: database user ('sqlserver' for dev)
    * `SQL_PASSWORD_DEV`: database password ('password generated by the browser when creating the instance' for dev)
    * `SQL_DB_NAME_DEV`: database name ('solar' for dev)

## Our Workspace Structure

### 1. Main App (main-app)
This is the main application, targteted at web browsers, based on Angular 19, standalone architecture, signals, no SSR.

### 2. Solar Lib (solar-lib)
Library containing the common angular entities. This will include:
* Common Components
* Directives
* Styles
* Typescript utilities
* General Services

### 3, 4, 5 - Server Access

### 3. Contract (contract)
Defines what the client expects from the server

### 4. Real API Server (real-api)***

### 5. Fake API Server (mock-api)
